ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for Nasi Goreng Paprik; closest parent 'fried rice food product' (FOODON_00002694) used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FAN (Fried rice) is the closest defensible parent category for Nasi Goreng Paprik; no dish-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Nasi Goreng Paprik or fried rice as a specific food concept at this level of specificity.

main_ingredients: paprik sauce (chilli, capsicum, oyster sauce, fish sauce)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Nasi Goreng Paprik is approximately 350 g, typically presented as a full plate with rice, protein, and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Goreng (Fried Rice) — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia, adapted for Paprik variant
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — MyFCD; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on the IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for Nasi Goreng (fried rice with egg and mixed protein) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Southeast Asian fried rice. Paprik variant includes additional chilli, capsicum, and oyster/fish sauce, which modestly elevates sodium and fat relative to a plain nasi goreng baseline. Values represent a best-estimate adaptation for the Paprik preparation style. Energy and macros are consistent with HPB Singapore data for comparable fried rice dishes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Fried rice (white rice, stir-fried with egg and mixed ingredients)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nasi Goreng Paprik specifically. GI of 64 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for fried rice (white rice base, stir-fried with egg and mixed ingredients), which is the closest analogous food. The Paprik variant uses the same white rice base and comparable cooking method; the addition of chilli and capsicum does not materially alter the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (64 × 89.25) / 100 ≈ 57.1, rounded to 57.3 using 25.5 g carb per 100 g × 350 g serving = 89.25 g carb.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce, oyster sauce, and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Nasi Goreng Paprik due to its high sodium content (approximately 480 mg per 100 g, equating to ~1680 mg per standard 350 g serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sauce preparation or smaller portions is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from white rice base and large serving size
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with Nasi Goreng Paprik, as the white rice base produces a medium glycemic index (GI 64) and a high glycemic load per standard serving (~57 GL units), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion reduction to 150–200 g and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat content from stir-frying oil and egg
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the elevated sodium (~480 mg/100 g) and moderate fat content (5.8 g/100 g) in Nasi Goreng Paprik; choosing preparations with less oil, reduced sauce, and lean protein (chicken breast or tofu) can help lower the cardiovascular risk profile of this dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Goreng Paprik is a Malaysian and Singaporean stir-fried rice dish distinguished by its bold, spicy-savoury paprik sauce, which is made from fresh chillies, capsicum (bell pepper), oyster sauce, fish sauce, and soy sauce. The dish is cooked in a wok over high heat, giving the rice a characteristic smoky, slightly charred flavour known as 'wok hei'. It is typically prepared with day-old cooked white jasmine rice, egg, garlic, onion, and a protein such as chicken, prawns, or mixed seafood. The word 'paprik' derives from the Thai-influenced Malay culinary tradition and refers to the chilli-capsicum sauce base rather than the spice paprika. The dish is commonly served on a banana leaf or plate, garnished with sliced cucumber, tomato, and a fried egg on top. Nutritionally, Nasi Goreng Paprik is a calorie-dense, carbohydrate-rich dish (approximately 175 kcal and 25.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g) with moderate protein (6.2 g/100 g) and fat (5.8 g/100 g). Its sodium content is notably high due to the multiple savoury sauces used in the paprik preparation, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is medium (GI ~64) but the glycemic load per standard serving is high, which is clinically relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is a popular hawker and restaurant dish in Singapore and Malaysia, available across Indian-Muslim, Malay, and mamak food establishments.
common_user_queries: How many calories in nasi goreng paprik?
What is the difference between nasi goreng and nasi goreng paprik?
Can diabetics eat nasi goreng paprik?
What sauce is used in nasi goreng paprik?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Nasi Goreng Paprik exists in any single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD fried rice entries and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for comparable Southeast Asian fried rice dishes, with adjustments for the paprik sauce components. GI is estimated from the closest analogous fried rice entry in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA fried rice data; no exact Nasi Goreng Paprik entry exists in any single approved source. Sodium is elevated relative to plain fried rice due to the multi-sauce paprik preparation (fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce). Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source databases for this food category. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the IMR MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source databases for this food category. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for stir-fried white rice with egg and mixed ingredients; no direct GI measurement for Nasi Goreng Paprik was found in the approved GI literature. GL per serving = (64 × 89.25) / 100 ≈ 57.3, based on 350 g serving containing 89.25 g carbohydrate. The dish is generally considered halal when prepared with halal-certified sauces and protein; kosher status is not applicable. FoodOn match type is closest_parent (FOODON_00002694, fried rice food product); FoodEx2 match type is closest_parent (A0FAN, Fried rice); no SNOMED CT code is applicable.
